title: T-093 — align packages/ui to React 19 peerDeps + reinstall zustand@5/react-hook-form (FT-033)
prompt:
# T-093 — align packages/ui to React 19 peerDeps + reinstall zustand@5/react-hook-form (FT-033)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.y
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-033** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P0** (M effort)

## Goal
packages/ui peerDeps: react ^19, react-dom ^19; reinstall zustand@5 + react-hook-form; apps/web imports Stepper + Field from @monopilot/ui successfully.

## Implementation contract
1. packages/ui/package.json peerDependencies: react ^19, react-dom ^19.
2. zustand@5 + react-hook-form@latest installed.
3. packages/ui/peerDependencies.md documents the policy.
4. apps/web/package.json sets matching React versions.
5. New vitest test in packages/ui mounts Stepper + Field under React 19 with vitest's React 19 react/jsx-runtime and asserts no peer-dep warnings.

## Files (scope)
- packages/ui/package.json [modify]
- packages/ui/peerDependencies.md [create]
- apps/web/package.json [modify]
- packages/ui/src/__tests__/react19-render.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given pnpm install runs, when complete, then `pnpm why react` from packages/ui shows React 19 satisfying the peer.
2. Given the new test, when run, then it mounts Stepper + Field without runtime errors and no React-peer warning is logged.
3. Given apps/web, when `pnpm typecheck` runs after the change, then no type errors related to @monopilot/ui primitives.
4. Given existing UI consumer tests, when run, then they pass after the upgrade.

## Test strategy
- RED: write react19-render.test.tsx mounting Stepper + Field under React 19 before the peerDep swap; should fail with the current React 18 peer.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test react19-render` and `pnpm typecheck`.

## Risk red lines
- P0 deploy blocker — do not merge without GREEN evidence on staging
- Do not retain dual React 18/19 peer ranges (forces consumers to ship both).
- Do not skip zustand@5 (React 19 compat).
- Do not paste prototype JSX wholesale into the test.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Align packages/ui peerDeps to React 19; reinstall zustand@5 and react-hook-form against React 19; verify Stepper/Field render in apps/web.
pipeline_inputs.details: apps/web is on React 19 but packages/ui still declares React 18 peers, which makes every primitive (Stepper, Field, Modal, etc.) unconsumable from apps/web without workarounds. T-037 had to ship `InlineStepper` and a custom jsxPreTransformPlugin. This task fixes the peerDeps and re-installs the affected transitive deps so the primitives become usable directly.
acceptance_criteria: Given pnpm install runs, when complete, then `pnpm why react` from packages/ui shows React 19 satisfying the peer.
Given the new test, when run, then it mounts Stepper + Field without runtime errors and no React-peer warning is logged.
Given apps/web, when `pnpm typecheck` runs after the change, then no type errors related to @monopilot/ui primitives.
Given existing UI consumer tests, when run, then they pass after the upgrade.
